description: Machine-readable runtime dependency allowlist for Handshake product code. Declares the only allowed classes of external runtime inputs (operator-provided model/tensor/CUI-portable artifacts, all operator-gated) and the forbidden runtime dependency classes (outside apps, external daemons, Docker-default paths, SQLite, Redis/Valkey, CDN-loaded runtime assets). Consumed by: app/src/lib/dependency_policy/allowlist.ts (typed accessor), app/scripts/lib/dependency_policy_scans.mjs (tripwire scanners), app/scripts/check-dependency-policy.mjs (validator hook, MT-032), app/scripts/generate-third-party-notices.mjs (MT-029), and src/backend/handshake_core/src/dependency_policy/mod.rs (Rust counterpart parity test). This file is product code, not governance: it gates what the shipped product may depend on at runtime.

allowed_external_runtime_inputs.description: Operator-provided GGUF model files loaded by the model runtime. Data artifacts only: never runtime authority, storage authority, or proof shortcuts (MT-023).
Operator-provided safetensors model weights loaded by the model runtime. Data artifacts only (MT-023).
Operator-provided tensor data artifacts (steering vectors, embeddings, adapters). Data artifacts only (MT-023).

CUI portable artifacts (operator-provided portable tool bundles). Allowed only where a separate owning WP explicitly gates them; the gate defaults closed (MT-022). WP-009 core editor/index/memory behavior must never depend on this class.

forbidden_runtime_dependency_classes.description: External desktop applications required for core product behavior. Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native.
Externally managed servers or daemons (Redis, Valkey, Memcached, external brokers) as runtime or cache authority. PostgreSQL+EventLedger managed by Handshake is the only durable-state authority.
Docker or containers required by default (non-opt-in) product paths. Container tooling is allowed only behind the explicitly opt-in sandbox isolation adapters listed in docker_opt_in_exceptions.
SQLite in any form (direct, transitive, wasm, node:sqlite) is forbidden in product code. PostgreSQL+EventLedger is canonical for durable state.

Runtime-loaded assets from CDNs. All editor/UI assets (Monaco, Tiptap, fonts, workers) must be bundled, lockfile-governed, and offline-capable. @monaco-editor/loader and @monaco-editor/react are forbidden because their default loader fetches Monaco from a CDN.

bundled_libraries.reason: Rich-text editor core and extensions for Loom documents (WP-009). Bundled, lockfile-governed product library.
Tiptap's underlying editor model and its small MIT helper packages.
Code editor surface. Bundled via the monaco-editor npm package with locally bundled Vite workers; the CDN-based @monaco-editor/loader is forbidden.
CRDT collaboration model and its MIT runtime helpers.
Statically compiled source parsers for AI-ready-data chunking. Grammars are compiled into the binary; no runtime .so/.wasm grammar downloads (MT-028).

docker_opt_in_exceptions.reason: Opt-in HardIsolation sandbox adapter. The default sandbox adapter is WSL2 podman (sandbox/bootstrap.rs); DockerAdapter::try_new is failure-tolerant, so every core product path works without Docker installed.

built_output_scan_exceptions.reason: Excalidraw 0.18 hardcodes a static ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL constant pointing at esm.sh inside its published bundle; the string cannot be removed without patching the library. Its core fonts are bundled by Vite into app/dist/assets (*.woff2 emitted at build), so the fallback stays dormant in the packaged product. The exception only suppresses esm.sh hits that sit within max_marker_distance characters of the literal ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL marker (self-verifying); any other esm.sh reference still fails the MT-027 built-output scan. Tracked risk for the canvas-owning WP: an Excalidraw surface relying on lazily fetched non-bundled assets would make this fallback live — that surface must pin window.EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH to a packaged path and prove offline canvas load with esm.sh aborted.
